$comment: SoT for the rail governance book-state keys (Phase G · G0, spec docs/specs/2026-07-15-agent-task-governance.md §14 D4). A rail step's `done_when: "<key> <op> <n>"` names one of these keys; the chat-service per-turn probe reads each key from an /internal route; the agent-registry seed authors done_when using them. THREE places drift silently unless machine-checked against ONE source — a renamed key or route disables the gate (reads as satisfied, or falls back to the call log). Both sides check THIS file: chat-service/tests/test_book_state_contract.py (BOOK_STATE_KEYS ⇄ probe routes ⇄ labels ⇄ BookState fields) and agent-registry migrate_lint_test.go (seeded done_when keys ⊆ this set). Change a key here + both consumers, or a test reds. Mirrors the contracts/frontend-tools.contract.json discipline for effects.

connections.label: cast members placed in the connection map

structure.label: arcs compiled into real chapter/scene structure
structure.$note: ensure-EXISTS — compile-attributed (plan_run_id set); EXCLUDES bare composition_arc_create. D3.

structure_fresh.label: arcs the latest plan run just compiled
structure_fresh.$note: produce-NEW — latest plan_run only; a re-plan reads 0 until ITS compile lands. D2.

suggestions.label: suggested items still waiting for review
